seo.title: How to Choose The Right Insurance ? |Mahavir Chopra
seo.description: Are You Making These Costly Insurance Mistakes? Most Indians are buying insurance completely wrong, and it could cost you your savings when your family

takeaways.text: Many claims get rejected for reasons set at the buying stage, so being transparent and accurate in your proposal form is what actually protects your family later.
Insurance in India is widely misunderstood, and treating a policy as risk protection rather than an investment changes which product you should pick.
ULIPs and endowment plans bundle cover with returns, but the episode's guidance is to keep protection and investing separate instead of chasing equity-like returns inside a policy.
Your buying channel matters, and the conversation weighs online versus agent and flags the problems with aggregators so you choose deliberately.
Before signing, understand your policy and don't overpay for coverage that doesn't actually serve your needs.

fork.decision: Should you buy insurance that also invests your money, or keep protection and investing separate?

points.text: ULIPs and endowment plans give you cover and a savings or investment component in one product
Some of these products are pitched as offering equity-like returns alongside protection
It feels convenient to handle protection and growth through a single policy
Term insurance is a pure risk management tool, giving more cover for less money
Keeping investing separate means you don't dilute either your protection or your returns
The episode's core advice is to focus on risk protection, not investment returns, and to avoid overpaying for bad coverage

fork.pick: Keep them separate. Buy term insurance for genuine protection, invest your money on its own, and don't let a bundled ULIP or endowment plan quietly weaken both your cover and your savings.
